title: Gaming PC deflects bullet shot through wall by neighbour, saving owner's life criminal negligence charges for culprit who claims 'firearm was accidentally discharged by her dog'
summary: A gaming PC's RAM sticks deflected a bullet shot through a wall, saving the owner's life. A neighbor's accidental firearm discharge led to the incident. The owner is grateful for their gaming PC's unexpected lifesaving intervention.
insight: Unlikely objects can sometimes provide unexpected lifesaving interventions in critical moments.

title: AI is set to consume up to 600 billion gallons of water by 2030 rising energy consumption primarily to blame as data center power demands rise
summary: Data centers are projected to consume up to 600 billion gallons of water by 2030, largely due to increasing energy demands from rising power consumption. This is primarily caused by the growing power needs of data center GPUs and rack systems. Direct cooling methods require significantly less water compared to traditional cooling methods.
insight: Rising energy consumption drives unsustainable water usage in data centers.

title: Massive 8TB SD cards are set to ship 'shortly' after a two-year delay mind-blowing storage at possibly bank-breaking prices
summary: 8TB SD cards are set to release after a two-year delay, offering massive storage capacity. The exact launch date and pricing remain unknown. Notebookcheck reports on the impending retail market arrival.
insight: Unprecedented storage capacity meets uncertain, potentially high pricing

title: We tested 20 wall chargers, from cheap to expensive, to find the best from 15W to 140W, here are the chargers that perform the best without overheating and throttling
summary: We tested 20 top wall chargers for performance and thermal control. Our results reveal the best chargers that deliver consistent power without overheating and throttling. These findings help consumers make informed purchasing decisions.
insight: Reliable charging speeds matter for seamless device performance and longevity.

title: Louis Rossmann is suing Samsung after firm offers $330 refund for defective SSD while selling the drives on Amazon for $949 spat over 4TB 990 Pro SSD is headed to court
summary: Louis Rossmann is suing Samsung over a defective 4TB 990 Pro SSD. Samsung offered a $330 refund under warranty, but Rossmann claims this is insufficient. The issue is now heading to court.
insight: Right to Repair movement challenges corporate control over repair and replacement.

title: Brad Paisley joins fight as zoo's dispute with AI data center escalates, petition tops 330,000 signatures Nashville weighs sweeping hyperscale ban
summary: A data center dispute near Nashville Zoo escalates with a zoning appeal filed, as country singer Brad Paisley lends his support and a petition surpasses 330,000 signatures, with Nashville considering a ban on hyperscale data centers.
insight: Community preservation vs. technological progress in Nashville's urban landscape.

title: Intel's upcoming Z970 and Z990 flagship chipsets will reportedly consume up to 14W at peak load, courtesy of more PCIe 5.0 support Nova Lake motherboards may feature a 22% smaller PCH than Z890
summary: Intel's Z990 PCH for Nova Lake motherboards is smaller than Z890, despite increased power consumption.
insight: Power efficiency trade-off in smaller, more powerful chipsets.

title: AMD fires back at Nvidia, claiming 256-core Zen 6 'Venice' CPU beats Vera by 3.3x in rack-level performance company shares first estimated EPYC Venice benchmarks
summary: AMD claims its 256-core EPYC Venice CPU outperforms Nvidia's Vera by 3.3x in rack-level deployments, showcasing AMD's advancements in high-performance computing.
insight: EPYC Venice provides significant performance boost over competing high-end solutions

title: Google reportedly books Intel for packaging more than 3 million TPUs in 2028 SK hynix is testing Intel's EMIB packaging for HBM integration
summary: Google orders Intel to build over 3 million TPUs in 2028 after testing advanced packaging. This comes after months of evaluation by Google of Intel's packaging capabilities. The deal is a significant boost for Intel's packaging business.
insight: Breakthrough packaging tech fuels large-scale AI computing deployment ambitions.

title: Samsung Heavy Industries recruits Greek shipowner and Supermicro to bring 50MW floating AI data centers to market can be powered by solid oxide fuel cells running on liquefied natural gas
summary: Samsung Heavy Industries partners with a Greek shipowner and Supermicro to develop 50MW floating AI data centers that can be powered by solid oxide fuel cells running on liquefied natural gas, a move that could revolutionize data storage and processing at sea.
insight: Transforming data storage with innovative, eco-friendly, and scalable sea-based solutions.

title: Valve to discontinue physical Steam gift cards by the end of 2026 due to scammers says nefarious actors continue to exploit them despite years of restrictions
summary: Steam will discontinue physical gift cards by the end of 2026, citing scams.
insight: Steam gift cards vulnerable to scammers despite security efforts.

title: Overenthusiastic GTA 6 fan claims to be monitoring oxygen levels, acoustic noise from the bushes at Rockstar North HQ promises trailer 3 launch is imminent based on heightened activity
summary: A GTA 6 fan claims to be monitoring Rockstar North HQ's activity, posting surveillance on Reddit to predict the next trailer. The fan's methods are likely a joke or a delusion. Their claims have sparked debate among the gaming community.
insight: Fanbase enthusiasm may lead to speculation and misinformation

title: Bare-metal MSX2+ Emulator for ESP32-S3 offers custom LCD_CAM VGA implementation & Z80 optimizations
summary: The S3-MSX-PC project offers a bare-metal MSX2+ emulator for ESP32-S3, featuring custom VGA output and Z80 optimizations. It uses a resistor ladder for 64-color VGA and Sigma-Delta modulation for audio. The emulator can run games at 60Hz on an off-the-shelf ESP32-S3 board.
insight: Retro gaming on ESP32-S3 with custom, high-quality video output

title: piBrick PocketCM5 An open-source handheld Linux computer kit for Raspberry Pi CM5
summary: The piBrick PocketCM5 is an open-source handheld Linux computer kit based on Raspberry Pi CM5, featuring a 3.92-inch AMOLED touchscreen display, physical keyboard, and expansion options. It's designed for developers, makers, and system administrators. This Linux machine is compact and versatile.
insight: Compact, open-source Linux machine for makers and developers

title: PZSDR P047 RF-ADC and RF-DAC high-end SDR board is based on AMD Zynq UltraScale+ ZU47DR RFSoC (Crowdfunding)
summary: The PZSDR P047 is a high-end SDR board featuring 8 RF ADCs and 8 RF DACs, based on the AMD Zynq UltraScale+ ZU47DR RFSoC. It includes various interfaces and a quad-core processor, making it suitable for advanced MIMO wireless applications. The board is available through crowdfunding.
insight: High-performance MIMO wireless applications now have a highly capable platform

title: Best of Sensors: Aizip and Asahi Kasei win in wearables
summary: AI Gesture Recognition, a wearable device, won Best of Sensors 2026 in the healthcare and wearable category. It uses wrist electrical signals for intuitive input commands, revolutionizing user interaction. This technology has significant potential for healthcare and accessibility.
insight: Revolutionizing user interaction with subtle electrical signals at the wrist.

title: California tests smart freeway that could make drivers wait four minutes to merge
summary: California's smart freeway on I-15 in Temecula aims to optimize traffic flow by controlling merge times, which could potentially make drivers wait four minutes to merge. This technology aims to reduce congestion and improve safety. It was launched on June 1.
insight: Smart freeway technology aims to optimize traffic flow and reduce congestion.

title: GM just updated 250,000 EVs to sell power back to the grid
summary: GM has updated 250,000 EVs to enable bidirectional charging, allowing owners to sell power back to the grid.
insight: Electric vehicles become grid energy storage and revenue sources simultaneously.

title: Canada introduces social media ban for under-16s, but platforms could avoid it
summary: Canada's Safe Social Media Act forces tech companies to protect under-16s from online harms.
insight: Tech companies' responsibility increases, shifting burden from parents.

title: The Best Laptops of 2026: A Disruptive Mac, a Windows Comeback, and an Nvidia Wildcard
summary: The 2026 laptop market has been impacted by a memory shortage, leading to increased prices. A $599 Apple laptop has bucked this trend. This has disrupted the market and sparked interest.
insight: Innovative products can thrive in times of scarcity and high demand.

title: AMD claims next-gen Zen 6 server CPU will deliver 330% of Nvidia Vera's performance per rack
summary: AMD claims its next-gen Zen 6 server CPU will outperform Nvidia Vera, citing dramatic improvements in performance. The projection is based on AMD's earlier benchmark results that favored Nvidia's processor. The company aims to deliver a significant advantage in server performance.
insight: AMD's Zen 6 CPU promises to revolutionize server computing performance

title: Windows Ready Print is Microsoft's biggest overhaul of Windows printing in years
summary: Microsoft's Windows Ready Print updates the company's printing model to align with modern communication standards.
insight: Enhanced interoperability between printing devices and the Windows ecosystem.

title: Logitech's new Mobi Fold mouse folds flat for travel
summary: Logitech's Mobi Fold mouse folds flat for travel, providing comfort and quiet use.
insight: Compact wireless mouse design simplifies travel storage options.

title: A developer ported Half-Life to a Nokia N95, and it runs at 30fps with Bluetooth keyboard support
summary: Developer Dante Leoncini ported Half-Life to Nokia N95, achieving 30fps at launch. This feat was made possible by modern open-source engines, allowing the 1998 PC game to run on a 2007 mobile device. The project supports Bluetooth keyboard input.
insight: Legacy games can be reimagined on new platforms with modern tech.

title: This soundbar can be hijacked over Bluetooth to control your computer, no pairing required
summary: A midrange soundbar can be hijacked via Bluetooth to control computers without pairing
insight: Bluetooth vulnerability allows unauthorized remote control of connected devices

title: A one-of-a-kind AMD PC signed by Lisa Su was sitting unopened in a basement for a decade
summary: A rare AMD PC signed by Lisa Su was discovered in a basement after a decade. The PC is believed to have been a retirement gift to Rory Read in 2014. Its significance and value remain uncertain.
insight: Rare tech memorabilia holds potential for significant monetary or historical value.

title: GPU-Z remains the go-to tool for detailed graphics card info
summary: GPU-Z provides a clean snapshot of GPU information without installation requirements. It supports various GPU manufacturers, including Nvidia, AMD, and Intel. The tool is useful for verifying specs, monitoring behavior, or troubleshooting.
insight: Accurate GPU information for troubleshooting and system optimization.

title: Experts say Donut Lab's "breakthrough" solid-state battery is just ordinary lithium-ion
summary: Donut Lab's solid-state battery is not a breakthrough, but a lithium-ion battery. This conclusion is based on testing data from VTT, which shows the battery's behavior aligns with lithium-ion chemistry. The company claimed it was a sodium-ion solid-state design.
insight: False marketing of a non-revolutionary battery technology matters

title: YouTuber says he will sue Samsung for dodging 990 Pro SSD warranty replacement amid memory crisis
summary: Rossmann claims Samsung is dodging warranty for the 990 Pro SSD due to memory crisis.
insight: Samsung's warranty policy may be impacted by global memory supply issues.

title: Crazy Taxi is back, but Sega's use of generative AI steals the spotlight
summary: Crazy Taxi: World Tour is coming in 2027 to multiple platforms, with Axel returning as the main driver. The game's fast-paced gameplay and San Francisco-style city are showcased in the latest footage. Fans can expect a new installment in the beloved franchise.
insight: Sega's use of generative AI dominates the Crazy Taxi revival buzz

title: SanDisk's massive 8TB SD cards are finally close to launch
summary: SanDisk's 8TB SD cards are nearing release, promising ample storage for portable devices.
insight: Massive storage capacity for portable devices is finally becoming more accessible.

title: Signal attacks UK plan to scan devices for nude images as "mass surveillance"
summary: The UK plans to scan devices for nude images via built-in features, sparking concerns over mass surveillance.
insight: Raises questions about balancing child protection with individual privacy rights.
